title: A cousin I rarely see
summary: Describe a cousin and recall the last time you spent time together.
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a cousin you do not see very often. Describe what they look like and what kind of person they are. Then describe the last time you saw them. Where were you? What did you do together? Use past simple and comparative adjectives. Use when, because, and although.
hintStarters: I have a cousin who lives far away…
The last time I saw her/him was…
Although we don't see each other often, we always…

hintVocab.meaningVi: trò chuyện cập nhật tin tức của nhau
hòa hợp, chơi tốt với nhau

sampleText: I have a cousin named Marco who lives in Genoa, in the north of Italy. We grew up in different cities, so we only saw each other at family weddings and Christmas gatherings. Marco is taller than me and has very dark hair that he keeps short. He is quieter than most people in our family and likes to listen more than he speaks. The last time I saw him was two years ago at my uncle's birthday party. At first it felt a little awkward because we had not spoken for almost a year. But after about an hour, we started to catch up properly and it felt like we had never been apart. We walked around the garden together and he told me about his new job and a trip he had taken to Scotland. I told him about my studies. When the party ended, we said we would call more often. We tried for a few months, but we slowly drifted apart again. I think about him sometimes. Although we are not close in the way cousins in films are, I always feel happy when I see him. Some family relationships are like that — quiet and distant, but still warm.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: lớn lên ở các thành phố khác nhau
đám cưới gia đình và những buổi họp mặt Giáng sinh
trầm lặng hơn hầu hết mọi người
thực sự cập nhật tin tức cho nhau

sampleAnnotations.phrase: felt like we had never been apart
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cảm giác như chưa bao giờ xa nhau

sampleAnnotations.phrase: not close in the way cousins in films are
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: không thân thiết theo kiểu anh chị em họ trong phim

sampleAnnotations.phrase: always feel happy when I see him
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: luôn cảm thấy vui khi gặp anh ấy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: quiet and distant, but still warm
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: lặng lẽ và xa cách, nhưng vẫn ấm áp

sampleAnnotations.phrase: had not spoken for almost a year
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: gần một năm không nói chuyện

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Comparative adjectives with 'than'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng tính từ so sánh kèm 'than' để so sánh hai người hoặc vật: 'taller than me', 'quieter than most people'. Tính từ ngắn thêm '-er'; tính từ dài dùng 'more + tính từ'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use comparative adjectives with 'than' to compare two people or things: 'taller than me', 'quieter than most people'. Short adjectives add '-er'; long adjectives use 'more + adjective'.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi có một người anh họ tên Marco sống ở Genova, miền bắc Italy. Chúng tôi lớn lên ở các thành phố khác nhau, nên chỉ gặp nhau tại đám cưới gia đình và những buổi họp mặt Giáng sinh. Marco cao hơn tôi và có mái tóc rất đen được cắt ngắn. Anh ấy trầm lặng hơn hầu hết mọi người trong gia đình và thích lắng nghe hơn là nói. Lần cuối tôi gặp anh là hai năm trước tại tiệc sinh nhật của chú tôi. Ban đầu cảm thấy hơi ngại ngùng vì gần một năm chúng tôi không nói chuyện. Nhưng sau khoảng một tiếng, chúng tôi bắt đầu thực sự cập nhật tin tức cho nhau và cảm giác như chưa bao giờ xa nhau. Chúng tôi đi dạo trong vườn và anh kể về công việc mới và chuyến đi đến Scotland. Tôi kể về việc học của mình. Khi tiệc kết thúc, chúng tôi hứa sẽ gọi điện thường xuyên hơn. Chúng tôi cố gắng vài tháng, nhưng rồi lại dần xa nhau. Đôi khi tôi nghĩ đến anh. Dù chúng tôi không thân thiết theo kiểu anh chị em họ trong phim, tôi luôn cảm thấy vui khi gặp anh. Một số mối quan hệ gia đình như vậy — lặng lẽ và xa cách, nhưng vẫn ấm áp.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct comparative: 'Marco is ___ than me and has very dark hair.'
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Tall' is a short adjective, so the comparative is 'taller'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'to slowly lose contact with someone'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Drift apart' means to gradually become less close to someone.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase describes slowly losing a connection?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means 'uncomfortable or socially difficult'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Awkward' means uncomfortable, especially in a social situation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct use of 'although' in this context:
options: Although we are not close, I always feel happy when I see him.
Although we are not close, but I always feel happy when I see him.
Although we are not close however I always feel happy when I see him.
Although we are not close. I always feel happy when I see him.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although' introduces a subordinate clause. No 'but' or 'however' is needed in the same sentence.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'although' at the start of one clause only — no 'but' needed.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means 'to talk and share news after a long time apart'?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Catch up' means to exchange news after not seeing someone for a while.
mcQuestions.hint: You do this when you meet someone you haven't seen in a long time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple: 'At first it ___ a little awkward.'
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'felt' is correct for a completed state in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: Use past simple for a past state or feeling.

options: When the party ended, we said we would call more often.
When the party ended, we are saying we would call more often.
When the party ends, we said we would call more often.
When the party ended, we have said we would call more often.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'When' + past simple followed by another past simple shows two connected past events.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs should be in past simple after 'when' for past events.

options: to call someone for the first time
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Stay in touch' means to keep communicating with someone over time.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase means 'to keep in contact'?

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'grown up' mean in 'We grew up in different cities'?
options: spent our childhood and became adults
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Grow up' means to pass through childhood and become an adult.
mcQuestions.hint: This describes the process from childhood to adulthood.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means to look similar to someone?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Resemble' means to look or seem like someone.
mcQuestions.hint: Which verb means 'to look like' someone?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you think distance makes family relationships stronger or weaker? What do you think is the best way to stay connected with relatives who live far away?
